title: The Three Billy Goats Gruff
content:
There were three billy goats named Gruff. The meadow where they lived had dry, thin grass. Across an old wooden bridge, a hillside was covered with the sweetest green grass. But under the bridge lived a big, mean troll.

The smallest Billy Goat Gruff went first. Trip-trap, trip-trap went his tiny hooves. "Who is that trip-trapping over my bridge?" roared the troll. "Only me, the smallest Billy Goat Gruff! Wait for my bigger brother. He is much tastier!" The troll let him pass.

Then the middle billy goat set off. Trip-trap, trip-trap! "Who goes there?" bellowed the troll. "Wait for my biggest brother! He is enormous!" said the middle goat. The greedy troll agreed to wait and let him pass too.

Then came the biggest Billy Goat Gruff. TRIP-TRAP! TRIP-TRAP! The whole bridge shook. "Who dares cross my bridge?" roared the troll. "It is I, the BIGGEST Billy Goat Gruff!" boomed the great goat. He lowered his horns and charged.

He caught the troll on his horns and flung him high into the air! The troll splashed into the river and was never seen again. All three goats crossed to the green hillside, ate the sweet grass, and grew fat and happy. Snip, snap, snout, this tale is told out!

author: Traditional
